title: Modernity Needs A Scapegoat
subtitle: A cleaned reading of Secret History #27
voice_policy: Direct argumentative prose derived from the lecture. Avoid repeated third-person framing. Do not impersonate first-person authorship except when quoting verified source material.
trace_policy: Paragraph-level transcript refs. Controversial historical, religious, ethnic, and institutional claims remain attributed to this dated source.
dek: A source-grounded reading of Jiang’s lecture on transnational capital, British sea empire, Frankist revolutionary theology, Disraeli’s Coningsby, Bolshevism, Marx, Bakunin, and Freud: modernity appears as a machine that hides capital, displays a scapegoat, turns truth into utility, turns objects into mirrors, and turns psychology into inward control.
thesis: The lecture is built around a dangerous reversal. The visible target is Jews, but Jiang’s stated model is that empire needs visible middle managers and scapegoats while transnational capital keeps profit in the shadows. British sea power must prevent a Eurasian heartland from unifying; to do that it sponsors chaos, revolution, debt, resource colonies, and useful diasporic networks. Frankist materialism and British imperial materialism then converge into modernity: liberalism, secularism, individualism, Darwinism, Marxism, and Freudian psychology. The strongest countercurrent is Bakunin: the object is not the mirror of freedom; another free person is. Paradise is not public property or self-analysis. It is love among people who recognize each other.

opening.heading: Core Reading
opening.text: This is not a simple “Jewish conspiracy” lecture in its own terms. Its first move is to say that conspiracy thinking is too easy. The sharper claim is that empire manufactures a face for power. Transnational capital hides, profits, and governs through trade, finance, debt, intelligence, revolution, and philosophy; Jews become the imperial middle managers who can be used, blamed, expelled, protected, or sacrificed. The argument then follows that machine through the British Empire, Ottoman debt, Disraeli’s strange literary self-disclosure, Bolshevik asset-stripping, Marx’s material paradise, Bakunin’s relational freedom, and Freud’s reversal from believing trauma to explaining it away.

marks.text: empire manufactures a face for power
Transnational capital hides, profits, and governs
the object is not the mirror of freedom; another free person is

beats.heading: The Empire Hides Behind Its Middle Managers
beats.summary: The opening gives the whole model: hidden capital, visible blame, British sea power, and the heartland nightmare.

paragraphs.text: The caveat comes first because the material is combustible. The lecture says it is easy to see a Jewish conspiracy everywhere, then turns the frame: Jews are not the ultimate empire but a construct and tool of empire. They are the middle managers and scapegoats. If they were not available, Jiang says, another group would have served the same imperial function.

marks.text: middle managers and scapegoats

paragraphs.text: The hidden actor is transnational capital. It gets the profit, hides behind the scenes, and leaves Jews with the blame. That is the episode’s first inversion: do not confuse the visible managerial layer with the shadow structure that owns the trade routes, banks, debt, and policy machinery.

marks.text: gets the profit, hides behind the scenes

paragraphs.text: The British Empire works because it controls sea lanes. The nightmare is a Eurasian heartland power able to link Europe, Asia, the Middle East, and Africa by land and rail. If that happens, the sea empire loses its toll booth. So British strategy becomes chaos: keep the French, Russians, Ottomans, Germans, and any future continental bloc fighting each other.

marks.text: the sea empire loses its toll booth
British strategy becomes chaos

paragraphs.text: The operational repertoire is subterfuge: finance revolutionary movements, industrialize Japan as a Russian counterweight, build resource colonies, and steal fast from India, China, and Africa. For that work, Jiang says the Frankist networks were useful because they were diasporic, wealthy, crypto-Jewish, revolutionary, and animated by the idea that the world must be destroyed to be saved.

marks.text: destroyed to be saved
diasporic, wealthy, crypto-Jewish, revolutionary

beats.heading: Debt Becomes Rule, Utility Replaces Truth
beats.summary: The Ottoman example turns alliance into debt control, then British philosophy turns truth into utility.

paragraphs.text: The Ottoman case shows empire as finance before it is battlefield conquest. Britain and France help the Ottomans against Russia, but the help comes through loans. Interest overwhelms the system; foreign bankers establish tax administration; a third of revenue flows directly to creditors. Sovereignty is lost through accounting.

marks.text: Sovereignty is lost through accounting
a third of revenue flows directly to creditors

paragraphs.text: When the Sultan turns to Germany, the Donmeh or Sabbatean-Frankist layer becomes useful again: finance the Young Turks, push secularism, liberalism, and individualism, and turn modernity into a revolutionary solvent. The claim is not merely that Turkey changes regimes; it is that modernity travels as an imperial tool.

marks.text: modernity travels as an imperial tool

paragraphs.text: The Rothschild Waterloo story gives the same model in miniature. Faster information creates panic; panic crashes assets; the insider buys the wreckage. War becomes a market for those who know first and care least about national loyalty.

marks.text: War becomes a market
know first and care least about national loyalty

paragraphs.text: The philosophical sequence is just as important. Locke’s blank slate, Hume’s skepticism, Bentham’s utility, and Mill’s liberalism move the world away from God, truth, and first principles. If truth cannot be reached, utility becomes the substitute god. Policy becomes math for pleasure, and liberalism becomes the moral language of that calculation.

marks.text: utility becomes the substitute god
Policy becomes math for pleasure

beats.heading: Disraeli Says The Quiet Part In A Novel
beats.summary: Jiang’s most volatile move is methodological: he reads Disraeli, a Jewish British prime minister, as having a Rothschild-like character voice hidden-power claims.

paragraphs.text: Darwin enters because the philosophy needs a science. Jiang’s objection is not that bodies cannot change; it is that Darwinism explains bodies while leaving the human imagination unexplained. In this reading, that omission is not a detail. It is the mind-versus-matter fault line.

marks.text: explains bodies while leaving the human imagination unexplained

paragraphs.text: Then comes Benjamin Disraeli’s Coningsby. Jiang’s point is source mechanics: the shocking claims are not introduced as an anti-Semite’s pamphlet but as a novel by a Jewish elite who later becomes British prime minister, with Sidonia presented as a Rothschild-like figure. That does not make the claims true. It makes the source strange enough that Jiang wants it read rather than waved away.

marks.text: source mechanics
read rather than waved away

paragraphs.text: The readings move through Christianity’s Jewish foundation, Spanish crypto-Jews, and the Inquisition as a surface institution hunting hidden Jews while, in the quoted story, hidden Jews occupy leadership. Jiang repeatedly marks the claim as something Disraeli is saying, not something he can simply verify.

marks.text: something Disraeli is saying
surface institution hunting hidden Jews

paragraphs.text: Sidonia’s world is cosmopolitan and stateless: information networks, war finance, migration to England, indifference to creed or country, and contact with agents and outcasts across Europe. Jiang contrasts this with Hegel, Homer, and Dante: great ideas do not float above a people. They grow out of belonging.

marks.text: great ideas do not float above a people
They grow out of belonging

paragraphs.text: The sharpest Coningsby line is the hidden-governance claim: the world is governed by very different people from those the public imagines. Jiang immediately adds the necessary brake: he does not think this is simply true. But he says the Bolshevik Revolution is the place where the evidence becomes hard to ignore.

marks.text: the world is governed by very different people
he does not think this is simply true

beats.heading: Revolution Is An Asset-Stripping Opportunity
beats.summary: Bolshevism becomes the example of revolution as terror, debt settlement, propaganda, and cheap purchase of a broken country.

paragraphs.text: The revolution model has four parts: a leader, mass appeal, organizational discipline, and incompetent enemies. Mao and Chiang Kai-shek are the quick example; Lenin and Trotsky are the Russian case. Lenin supplies fanatical focus, Trotsky supplies charisma and networks, and the Bolsheviks win despite limited electoral support.

marks.text: leader, mass appeal, organizational discipline, and incompetent enemies
fanatical focus

paragraphs.text: Red Terror is described as politics plus extraction. Kill opponents, terrorize families, seize hidden aristocratic wealth, hire mercenaries, and finance the war machine. The provocation is that revolutionary violence also has a balance sheet.

marks.text: revolutionary violence also has a balance sheet

paragraphs.text: Jiang rejects the classroom story that Allied intervention proves capitalism opposed Bolshevism. In his reading, armies entered because debts had to be paid, and the Bolsheviks paid by looting Russia. Anglo-American foreign policy serves private capital, not national interest; capital cares about profit, not ideology.

marks.text: capital cares about profit, not ideology
foreign policy serves private capital

paragraphs.text: The Richard Spence reading supplies the documentary frame: Wall Street pitchmen sell Bolshevik Russia as democratic, orderly, and good for business. War communism then reduces the economy to barter and robbery, with palaces, churches, museums, and bank vaults stripped and fenced abroad for hard currency. Empire destroys nations in order to buy the wreckage.

marks.text: Empire destroys nations in order to buy the wreckage

paragraphs.text: Trotsky becomes the scapegoat inside the revolution. Jiang is careful here: Trotsky did not, in this telling, work for transnational capital. But once capital refuses to rebuild Russia, the accusation fits his Jewish identity, New York support, and aloof personality. Stalin can turn that suspicion into power.

marks.text: Trotsky becomes the scapegoat inside the revolution
Trotsky did not... work for transnational capital

beats.heading: The Object Mirror Versus The Human Mirror
beats.summary: The lecture’s best philosophical passage contrasts Marx’s material object with Bakunin’s relational freedom.

paragraphs.text: Marx is introduced through Engels’s support and through the question of why capitalist money would support communism. Jiang’s answer is that communism benefits transnational capital by moving the debate. Instead of spirit versus flesh, everyone argues about private property versus public property. Either way, the frame is materialist.

marks.text: spirit versus flesh
private property versus public property

paragraphs.text: The attractive Marx is still allowed to be attractive. Capitalism degrades individuality through specialization. In a humane world, the maker creates a vase, sees his personality objectified in it, gives it to another person, and sees his impact reflected back. The object becomes a mirror.

marks.text: The object becomes a mirror

paragraphs.text: But that is the trick. Marx takes religious needs and routes them through matter. The object replaces God and community as the site where the self becomes real. Public property then becomes a material paradise, led by a vanguard elite. Jiang hears Jacob Frank in that structure: a new people leading everyone into paradise.

marks.text: religious needs routed through matter
a new people leading everyone into paradise

paragraphs.text: Bakunin is the rescue. He agrees with much of the socialist critique but rejects the vanguard because it will become a new capitalist class. A socialist who does not trust everyone’s capacity for freedom has only rebuilt empire with a different flag.

marks.text: rebuilt empire with a different flag

paragraphs.text: The crucial distinction is mirror versus mirror. Marx says the object reflects the self. Bakunin says the other free person reflects the self. Freedom is confirmed in the equally free consciousness of other people. Paradise comes through love, not objects, public property, socialism, or inward self-display.

marks.text: the other free person reflects the self
Paradise comes through love

paragraphs.text: That is why scientific administration is not salvation. Bakunin warns that if science alone governs social life, life withers into a voiceless and servile herd. Jiang reads Marxist vanguard rule, transnational capital, and British empire as the same slavery machine wearing different costumes.

marks.text: a voiceless and servile herd
the same slavery machine wearing different costumes

beats.heading: Psychology Turns The Wound Inward
beats.summary: Freud begins as a scientist who believes trauma, then becomes the example of modernity gaslighting its victims and turning attention inward.

paragraphs.text: Early Freud is presented sympathetically. Women report anxiety, depression, relational fear, and sleeplessness; Freud’s early conclusion is that real childhood sexual trauma caused the symptoms. The readings from The Etiology of Hysteria emphasize sexual trauma, repeated patient testimony, dissociation, and details too subtle to dismiss as fantasy.

marks.text: real childhood sexual trauma caused the symptoms
details too subtle to dismiss as fantasy

paragraphs.text: The student question is useful because Jiang narrows the claim. Upper-class cases appear more often not because abuse only happens among the rich, but because rich families can send daughters to psychiatrists and educated women are more likely to report. The class signal is an access-to-reporting signal.

marks.text: an access-to-reporting signal

paragraphs.text: Then the reversal: Jiang says Freud allies with the Frankists and changes the theory. The abused girl becomes the fantasist. The wound becomes masturbation, resentment, seduction fantasy, and the Oedipal complex. What Jiang calls complete nonsense becomes mainstream academic knowledge for decades.

marks.text: The abused girl becomes the fantasist
mainstream academic knowledge for decades

paragraphs.text: The next move is darker. Illness becomes a weapon for controlling family; dream analysis becomes mind control because dreams are pliable enough for suggestion; psychology becomes gaslighting. Freud is rewarded, protected by Britain, and systematized through Jung into a larger theory of mind.

marks.text: dream analysis becomes mind control
psychology becomes gaslighting

paragraphs.text: The closing returns to Bakunin. The modern mind looks inward and learns nothing because meaning is not inside the isolated self. Meaning, purpose, love, and freedom are found in each other. That is why the final question is framed as speculation, not certainty: are Marxism, liberalism, individualism, Darwinism, and psychology psyops meant to turn people into slaves? Jiang says he does not know. The lecture ends by handing the question to the listener and setting up Pax Judaica as the next culmination.

marks.text: meaning is not inside the isolated self
Jiang says he does not know
the question to the listener

questions.question: So you mentioned that this kind of rape is frequently occurred in upper class. Why does it not so frequently in the lower class?
questions.answer: Jiang answers that the evidence does not prove abuse only happens among the rich. Upper-class women are more likely to report because they have education, money, and access to psychiatrists; poorer women may have less ability to report or receive treatment.

source_notes.note: This episode contains unusually combustible claims about Jews, Frankists, Freemasons, communism, Freud, and transnational capital. The read preserves Jiang’s own caveats where he says some claims are source readings, theories, or uncertain connections rather than settled proof.
Several names are ASR-damaged in the transcript. “Buchanan” is treated as Bakunin from context; Sabbatean, Donmeh, and Frankist terms are also inconsistently rendered by ASR.
